_README: Known Keeta mainnet addresses used to classify transactions that would otherwise be mis-reported. Every entry MUST carry a `source` and a `verified` flag. Nothing goes in here unverified-and-unmarked. See FINDINGS.md 'Gate 3' for method.

_sources.live-metadata: https://static.network.keeta.com/metadata/services (fetched 2026-07-19; enumerated all keeta_ addresses by walking the JSON)
_sources.network-constants: https://theanchorfactory.com/docs/reference/network-constants (AnchorFactory, Jun 28 2026, v1) — local copy Desktop/keeta/network constants.pdf
_sources.observed-onchain: Derived from output/history-raw.json dumps of real mainnet accounts. Identity INFERRED from on-chain behaviour, not asserted by any registry.

bridgeAnchors.name: bivo-anchor.keeta.com
bridgeAnchors.note: Declared as the anchor's own account in the live service-discovery metadata. Cross-chain bridge (asset movement) provider.

bridgeAnchors.name: keeta (first-party asset movement)
bridgeAnchors.note: Declared as the anchor account for the 'keeta' assetMovement service. Bridges to Base/EVM per its locationMetadata.

otherAnchors.name: storage-area1.keeta.com
otherAnchors.note: NOT a bridge. Storage anchor. Listed so it is not mistaken for one; storage data is off-chain so it should generate no financial operations.

swapInfrastructure.name: unknown swap counterparty / market maker
swapInfrastructure.note: UNVERIFIED IDENTITY. Observed building the SEND+RECEIVE leg of real swaps in staple 74F743C3... Behaviour is consistent with an AMM/market maker. Not named in any registry. Do NOT treat as a bridge.

swapInfrastructure.name: unknown swap fee/routing recipient
swapInfrastructure.note: UNVERIFIED IDENTITY. Received three same-staple KTA sends (0.07, 0.077, 0.05) alongside a swap, then fanned 0.00206 KTA out to four further addresses. Behaviour is consistent with a routing/aggregator fee collector. These sends are part of the trade and must NOT be emitted as standalone withdrawals.

tokens.note: Base token. Decimals CONFIRMED against explorer.keeta.com — see FINDINGS.md Gate 1. NOTE: 9 decimals on testnet.
ZERO decimals. Must be distinguishable from 'missing'.

unknownTokensObserved.note: NOT in any registry. Symbol and decimals INFERRED: explorer.keeta.com rendered raw 24902188797697885934532853 as 24,902,188.797697885934532853 MURF, which implies 18 decimals. On-chain account info has EMPTY name and EMPTY metadata. This is the canonical example of a token v1 must HARD-FAIL on rather than guess.

unknownTokensObserved._note: Eight further non-KTA tokens were observed in the c24i dump, all with empty name and empty metadata. Addresses are in output/history-raw.json summary.distinctTokens. None are resolvable on-chain. v1 must refuse them.

_limitations: This list is NOT exhaustive and cannot be. Only TWO bridge anchors are registry-declared. Any bridge operating outside the live metadata endpoint is invisible to us.
A send to an address NOT on this list is therefore NOT evidence that it was not a bridge. The absence of a match must never be reported to the user as 'confirmed not a bridge'.
Consequently the pattern-based fallback in the spec is mandatory, not optional: v1 must tell the user plainly that bridge detection is best-effort.
